design_philosophy.core_loop: Traverse stages as Harry the carpenter, using a hammer to defeat enemies blocking your path, collect items, and reach the boss to progress
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Opposition from Rusty Nail Inc. and their construction company minions attempting to destroy the player's hometown of Beranme
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as a working-class carpenter hero defending his community against corporate destruction through direct combat action
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Thematically grounded protagonist (carpenter with hammer tool as weapon) rather than traditional martial artist; Japanese working-class heroism framing; localized significantly for Western audiences suggesting developer awareness of cultural context

difficulty.lives_system: Multiple lives per stage with loss announcements
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based progression with stage clear voice announcements

progression.upgrade_system: Consumable powerups found during stages (regional variants: sake/hot pepper for invincibility or similar; rice ball/cheese for health)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Stage-by-stage linear progression through themed levels
replayability_hooks: Multiple game series entries suggest speedrun/optimization potential
Stage-based structure allows replaying individual levels
Score/performance tracking through stage clear announcements

world_building_techniques: Clear protagonist motivation: Harry protecting his town against Rusty Nail Inc. corporate destruction
Character-driven narrative framing (carpenter defending home vs. faceless corporation)
Regional localization of visual elements (sake→hot pepper, onigiri→cheese) suggesting environmental storytelling adaptation
Title screen and stage presentation convey Japanese working-class cultural context

boss_design.philosophy: Final confrontation against Rusty Nail Inc. leadership as narrative climax to stage progression

faction_indicators: Harry (protagonist carpenter) vs. Rusty Nail Inc. (antagonist construction company)
Working-class hero framing vs. corporate villain framing
danger_signals: Construction company actively destroying residential areas (Harry's house torn down)
Organized opposition throughout town suggests systemic corporate threat
environmental_storytelling: Hometown of Beranme under threat from development/demolition
Progression through town areas damaged or controlled by Rusty Nail Inc.
Carpenter protagonist uses labor tool (hammer) as weapon, grounding fantasy in working-class reality

sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor: Hammerin' Harry (NES) regional differences documentation
wikipedia - Hammerin' Harry series overview and game list
